description_long: This is a seated stone statue depicting a human figure, likely of ancient Egyptian origin, as suggested by the headdress and stylized features. The figure sits upright on a blocky, high-backed throne with arms resting on the knees and feet flat on a rectangular base. The back and sides of the throne are inscribed with vertical lines of hieroglyphic text. The statue is rendered in a dark, fine-grained stone, giving it a solid and monumental appearance. The facial features are idealized and symmetrical, with a serene expression and a false beard, typical of pharaonic representations. The entire sculpture rests on a slightly larger plinth, which appears to be part of its display or installation.
description: A dark stone statue of a seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style, with hieroglyphic inscriptions on the throne and a serene, idealized face.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the back of the headdress, the high-backed throne, and vertical hieroglyphic inscriptions.
description_view_1: Front three-quarter view highlighting the face, chest, arms resting on knees, and the feet placed together on the base.
description_view_2: Opposite front three-quarter view, similar to view 1 but from the other side, showing the side of the throne and the figure's profile.
description_view_3: Rear three-quarter view from the opposite side of view 0, again emphasizing the back of the throne and inscriptions.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a plinth, suggesting an outdoor or indoor public exhibition space, but no specific environmental context is visible.
